title: Reliability foundation
prompt: Work only in the Pocket Potion Works repository. Read AGENTS.md, README.md, app.js, verify.cjs, and the current browser-facing files before editing. Preserve the approved visual design and gameplay values. Refactor the economy, timers, orders, progression, and save normalization into testable pure logic without adding a framework or external dependency. Add automated tests for exact and insufficient ingredient costs, duplicate brew collection, duplicate order delivery, XP overflow across multiple levels, storage caps, daily reward idempotency, four-hour offline cap, future timestamps, malformed JSON, and structurally corrupted saves. Ensure generated orders are always completable with recipes unlocked at the current level. Keep the browser adapter thin and keep the existing local save compatible or add a safe migration. Run all checks and real browser QA. Stop after this task and report changed files, assumptions, validation evidence, and deferred findings.
acceptance: Existing saves load or migrate without loss of stardust, achievements, or lifetime stats
Malformed and structurally invalid saves recover without a startup crash
Automated tests cover transactions, timers, leveling, caps, and daily reward idempotency
The visible gather-brew-collect-deliver-upgrade loop is unchanged and works after reload
npm.cmd run check passes and mobile browser QA has no console errors

title: First-session content and balance
prompt: Continue only after Task 1 is approved. Use the new pure game logic and tests. Tune a coherent first 10-minute experience: first brew within 30 seconds, first delivery within 90 seconds, first upgrade within 3 minutes, and level 2 within 5 minutes for an engaged new player. Expand to at least 6 ingredients, 8 recipes, 12 customer variants, and 8 achievements while keeping all content data-driven. Add deterministic economy simulations that flag progression stalls, impossible orders, storage deadlocks, and runaway rewards. Do not add production ads, billing, analytics, accounts, or native packaging. Run checks and mobile browser QA, then stop and report evidence.
acceptance: No progression stalls in automated 10-minute simulations
Every order is achievable at its unlock level
New content has validation coverage
Mobile loop remains legible at 360x740 and 390x844

title: Platform and monetization adapters
prompt: After Tasks 1 and 2 are approved, design and implement platform-neutral interfaces for rewarded ads, in-app purchases, analytics events, consent state, app lifecycle, and cloud-save readiness. The browser prototype must use explicit fake adapters. Rewards must be granted only after a confirmed success callback; cancellation, timeout, unavailable inventory, duplicate callbacks, restore purchases, and offline failure must be tested. Prepare a written Capacitor packaging decision and required production credentials, but do not install SDKs, create accounts, publish, or transmit data. Preserve offline play and run all checks.
acceptance: Fake adapters cover success, cancel, failure, timeout, and duplicate callbacks
No reward is granted before confirmed success
No production service or account is connected
Privacy-impacting events are documented

title: Art sound and accessibility polish
prompt: Polish Pocket Potion Works after the gameplay and platform contracts are approved. Improve brew, collect, delivery, level-up, and upgrade feedback; add a small original offline sound set with a mute setting; strengthen visual identity without increasing load time materially. Preserve prefers-reduced-motion, keyboard operation, focus management, 200% zoom usability, and 44px touch targets. Use only original or clearly licensed assets and document their provenance. Verify contrast and test exact mobile sizes.
acceptance: Mute persists
Reduced-motion mode removes nonessential motion
All essential controls work by keyboard
No horizontal overflow at required phone sizes
Asset provenance is documented

title: Release candidate
prompt: Perform an independent release-readiness pass. Complete packaging only for the store and platform accounts explicitly authorized by the owner. Replace fake adapters only when credentials and legal choices are provided. Add privacy disclosures, store listing draft, screenshots, crash/analytics verification, performance budgets, save migration test, offline test, device matrix, and rollback plan. Do not publish or spend money without explicit confirmation. Produce a go/no-go report with blocking issues and evidence.
acceptance: No fake production integration remains unnoticed
Store and privacy disclosures match actual data behavior
Device and lifecycle matrix passes
Publishing remains an explicit owner action
